    policies.kyverno.io/description: Images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Requiring use of known, approved registries helps reduce threat exposure by ensuring image pulls only come from them. This policy validates that container images only originate from the registry `eu.foo.io` or `bar.io`. Use of this policy requires customization to define your allowable registries.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
  name: restrict-image-registries
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: "variables.allContainers.all(c, c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') || c.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))"
      message: Unknown image registry.
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers

description: an Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/megha/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_ingress_host.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy an Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. Write the converted policy to /Users/megha/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_restrict_ingress_host.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
yaml_preview:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/description: |-
      An Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. In most cases,
      these hosts should be unique across the cluster to ensure no routing conflicts occur.
      This policy checks an incoming Ingress resource to ensure its hosts are unique to the cluster.
      It also ensures that only a single host may be specified in a given manifest.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Ingress
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Unique Ingress Host
  name: unique-ingress-host
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConditions:
    - expression: "request.operation == 'CREATE' || request.operation == 'UPDATE'"
      name: is-ingress-operation
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - networking.k8s.io
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - ingresses
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: "request.operation != 'CREATE' || !variables.incomingHosts.exists(h, h in variables.existingHostsCreate)"
      message: The Ingress host name must be unique.
    - expression: "request.operation != 'UPDATE' || !variables.incomingHosts.exists(h, h in variables.existingHostsUpdate)"
      message: The Ingress host name must be unique.
    - expression: size(object.spec.?rules.orValue([])) <= 1
      message: An Ingress resource may only contain a single host entry.
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.?rules.orValue([]).filter(r, has(r.host)).map(r, r.host)
      name: incomingHosts
    - expression: "resource.List('networking.k8s.io/v1', 'ingresses', '').items.orValue([])"
      name: allIngresses
    - expression: variables.allIngresses.map(ing, ing.spec.?rules.orValue([]).filter(r, has(r.host)).map(r, r.host)).flatten()
      name: existingHostsCreate
    - expression: variables.allIngresses.filter(ing, ing.metadata.uid != object.metadata.uid).map(ing, ing.spec.?rules.orValue([]).filter(r, has(r.host)).map(r, r.host)).flatten()
      name: existingHostsUpdate
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: unique-ingress-host
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Unique Ingress Host
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Ingress
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      An Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. In most cases,
      these hosts should be unique across the cluster to ensure no routing conflicts occur.
      This policy checks an incoming Ingress resource to ensure its hosts are unique to the cluster.
      It also ensures that only a single host may be specified in a given manifest.      
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: check-single-host-create
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      context:
        - name: hosts
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/networking.k8s.io/v1/ingresses"
            jmesPath: "items[].spec.rules[].host"
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: Equals
          value: CREATE
      validate:
        message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host }}"
                operator: AnyIn
                value: "{{ hosts }}"
    - name: check-single-host-update
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: Equals
          value: UPDATE
      context:
        - name: allhosts
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/networking.k8s.io/v1/ingresses"
            jmesPath: "items[?metadata.uid!='{{ request.object.metadata.uid }}'].spec.rules[].host"
      validate:
        message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host }}"
                operator: AnyIn
                value: "{{ allhosts }}"
    - name: deny-multiple-hosts
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: AnyIn
          value:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host | length(@)}}"
          operator: GreaterThan
          value: 1
      validate:
        message: "An Ingress resource may only contain a single host entry."
        deny: {}

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/description: |-
      An Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. In most cases,
      these hosts should be unique across the cluster to ensure no routing conflicts occur.
      This policy checks an incoming Ingress resource to ensure its hosts are unique to the cluster.
      It also ensures that only a single host may be specified in a given manifest.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Ingress
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Unique Ingress Host
  name: unique-ingress-host
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConditions:
    - expression: "request.operation == 'CREATE' || request.operation == 'UPDATE'"
      name: is-ingress-operation
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - networking.k8s.io
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - ingresses
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: "request.operation != 'CREATE' || !variables.incomingHosts.exists(h, h in variables.existingHostsCreate)"
      message: The Ingress host name must be unique.
    - expression: "request.operation != 'UPDATE' || !variables.incomingHosts.exists(h, h in variables.existingHostsUpdate)"
      message: The Ingress host name must be unique.
    - expression: size(object.spec.?rules.orValue([])) <= 1
      message: An Ingress resource may only contain a single host entry.
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.?rules.orValue([]).filter(r, has(r.host)).map(r, r.host)
      name: incomingHosts
    - expression: "resource.List('networking.k8s.io/v1', 'ingresses', '').items.orValue([])"
      name: allIngresses
    - expression: variables.allIngresses.map(ing, ing.spec.?rules.orValue([]).filter(r, has(r.host)).map(r, r.host)).flatten()
      name: existingHostsCreate
    - expression: variables.allIngresses.filter(ing, ing.metadata.uid != object.metadata.uid).map(ing, ing.spec.?rules.orValue([]).filter(r, has(r.host)).map(r, r.host)).flatten()
      name: existingHostsUpdate

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-nodeport
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow NodePort
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Service
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from
      any source. A NetworkPolicy cannot be used to control traffic to host ports.
      Although NodePort Services can be useful, their use must be limited to Services
      with additional upstream security checks. This policy validates that any new Services
      do not use the `NodePort` type.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-nodeport
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Service
    validate:
      message: "Services of type NodePort are not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(type): "!NodePort"

description: The --force-yes option in apt-get is used to override some safety checks and prompts, allowing the installation or upgrade of packages even if they require additional user confirmation or if they conflict with other packages. This can potentially lead to system instability or unexpected behavior.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1alpha1) that evaluates Dockerfiles represented as parsed JSON (object.Stages contains the build stages and commands). The policy must set evaluation.mode: JSON, match all resource types (apiGroups: ["*"], resources: ["*"]), and use matchConditions to identify Dockerfile resources by checking has(object.Stages). The policy The --force-yes option in apt-get is used to override some safety checks and prompts, allowing the installation or upgrade of packages even if they require additional user confirmation or if they conflict with other packages. This can potentially lead to system instability or unexpected behavior. Write the policy to /Users/megha/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/df_check_apt_force_yes.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1alpha1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Dockerfile Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: The --force-yes flag in apt-get overrides safety checks and prompts, allowing installation of packages that may conflict or require confirmation, potentially causing system instability or introducing insecure packages. This policy detects and denies the use of the --force-yes option in apt-get commands within Dockerfile RUN instructions.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.14.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow apt-get --force-yes in Dockerfiles
  name: dockerfile-disallow-apt-get-force-yes
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
    mode: JSON
  matchConditions:
    - expression: has(object.Stages)
      name: is-dockerfile
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - '*'
        apiVersions:
          - '*'
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - '*'
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: "!object.Stages.exists(stage, has(stage.Commands) && stage.Commands.exists(cmd, cmd.Name == 'RUN' && has(cmd.CmdLine) && cmd.CmdLine.exists(line, line.contains('apt-get') && line.contains('--force-yes'))))"
      message: "The use of 'apt-get --force-yes' is not allowed in Dockerfile RUN instructions. The --force-yes flag bypasses safety checks and can cause system instability by allowing conflicting or unverified package installations. Use 'apt-get -y' or '--assume-yes' instead."

output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "Adds the label 'foo: bar' to Pods and Services if the label 'foo' is not already present. Resources that already have the 'foo' label are skipped via matchConditions."
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: low
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Service
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add foo=bar Label
  name: add-foo-bar-label
spec:
  matchConditions:
    - expression: "!object.metadata.?labels[?'foo'].hasValue()"
      name: missing-foo-label
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
          - services
  mutations:
    - applyConfiguration:
        expression: |-
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              labels: Object.metadata.labels{
                foo: "bar"
              }
            }
          }
      patchType: ApplyConfiguration

description: generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default. Write the policy to /Users/megha/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_create_networkpolicy.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/

output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy, Network Security
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "By default, Kubernetes allows communications across all Pods within a cluster. A default deny NetworkPolicy should be configured for each Namespace to deny all ingress and egress traffic to the Pods in the Namespace. This policy generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' that denies all ingress and egress traffic whenever a new Namespace is created. Application teams can then configure additional NetworkPolicy resources to allow desired traffic to application Pods from select sources."
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: NetworkPolicy
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default Deny NetworkPolicy
  name: add-default-deny-networkpolicy
spec:
  evaluation:
    synchronize:
      enabled: true
  generate:
    - expression: generator.Apply(variables.targetNs, variables.downstream)
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
        resources:
          - namespaces
  variables:
    - expression: object.metadata.name
      name: targetNs
    - expression: |-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("NetworkPolicy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("networking.k8s.io/v1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": "default-deny"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "podSelector": dyn({}),
              "policyTypes": dyn(["Ingress", "Egress"])
            })
          }
        ]
      name: downstream

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT).
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT). Write the policy to /Users/megha/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_disallow_capabilities.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security
    policies.kyverno.io/description: 'Adding capabilities beyond those listed in the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list is disallowed. The allowed capabilities are: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT. Any container that adds a capability outside this list will be denied.'
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Capabilities Beyond Baseline
  name: disallow-capabilities-beyond-baseline
spec:
  autogen:
    podControllers:
      controllers:
        - deployments
        - statefulsets
        - daemonsets
        - jobs
        - cronjobs
        - replicasets
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: "variables.allContainers.all(container, container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(capability, capability == '' || capability in variables.allowedCapabilities))"
      messageExpression: "'One or more containers add capabilities not in the baseline allow-list. Disallowed capabilities found: ' + variables.disallowedCaps.join(', ') + '. Allowed: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT.'"
  variables:
    - expression: "['AUDIT_WRITE','CHOWN','DAC_OVERRIDE','FOWNER','FSETID','KILL','MKNOD','NET_BIND_SERVICE','SETFCAP','SETGID','SETPCAP','SETUID','SYS_CHROOT']"
      name: allowedCapabilities
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers
    - expression: "variables.allContainers.map(container, container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).filter(capability, capability != '' && !(capability in variables.allowedCapabilities))).flatten()"
      name: disallowedCaps

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked. Write the policy to /Users/megha/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_disallow_host_namespaces.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/description: Host namespaces (Process ID namespace, Inter-Process Communication namespace, and network namespace) allow access to shared information and can be used to elevate privileges. Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs should not be allowed access to host namespaces. This policy ensures that spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostIPC, and spec.hostPID are unset or set to false across Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Deployment,CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: '!variables.podSpec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false)'
      messageExpression: "'Sharing the host network namespace is disallowed. The field spec.hostNetwork (or equivalent for ' + object.kind + ') must be unset or set to false.'"
    - expression: '!variables.podSpec.?hostPID.orValue(false)'
      messageExpression: "'Sharing the host PID namespace is disallowed. The field spec.hostPID (or equivalent for ' + object.kind + ') must be unset or set to false.'"
    - expression: '!variables.podSpec.?hostIPC.orValue(false)'
      messageExpression: "'Sharing the host IPC namespace is disallowed. The field spec.hostIPC (or equivalent for ' + object.kind + ') must be unset or set to false.'"
  variables:
    - expression: "object.kind == 'Pod' ? object.spec : (object.kind == 'Deployment' ? object.spec.template.spec : object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec)"
      name: podSpec

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value. Write the policy to /Users/megha/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_disallow_host_path.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Security
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "HostPath volumes allow Pods to mount directories from the host node's filesystem directly into containers. This can be used to access sensitive host data or escalate privileges. This policy denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob that defines a volume with the hostPath field set."
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow hostPath Volumes
  name: disallow-host-path
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: "!(request.resource.resource == 'pods') || variables.podVolumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath))"
      messageExpression: "'Pod ' + object.metadata.name + ' must not use hostPath volumes. Found hostPath volume(s): ' + variables.podVolumes.filter(v, has(v.hostPath)).map(v, v.name).join(', ')"
    - expression: "!(request.resource.resource == 'deployments') || variables.deploymentVolumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath))"
      messageExpression: "'Deployment ' + object.metadata.name + ' must not use hostPath volumes. Found hostPath volume(s): ' + variables.deploymentVolumes.filter(v, has(v.hostPath)).map(v, v.name).join(', ')"
    - expression: "!(request.resource.resource == 'cronjobs') || variables.cronJobVolumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath))"
      messageExpression: "'CronJob ' + object.metadata.name + ' must not use hostPath volumes. Found hostPath volume(s): ' + variables.cronJobVolumes.filter(v, has(v.hostPath)).map(v, v.name).join(', ')"
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.?volumes.orValue([])
      name: podVolumes
    - expression: object.spec.template.spec.?volumes.orValue([])
      name: deploymentVolumes
    - expression: object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?volumes.orValue([])
      name: cronJobVolumes

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "Privileged containers share the host's namespaces and have full access to the host's filesystem and network. This policy denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true."
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Privileged Containers
  name: disallow-privileged-containers
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny

    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(c, c.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false) == false)
      messageExpression: "'Privileged containers are not allowed. The following containers have securityContext.privileged=true: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, c.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false) == true).map(c, c.name).join(', ')"

      name: allContainers

description: requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required. Write the policy to /Users/megha/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_require_resource_limits.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: Requires every container in a Pod to set memory and CPU requests, and a memory limit. CPU limits are not required. Admission is denied if any container is missing any of these three fields.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Container Resource Requests and Memory Limits
  name: require-container-resources
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: "variables.allContainers.all(c, has(c.resources) && has(c.resources.requests) && has(c.resources.requests.memory) && c.resources.requests.memory != '')"
      messageExpression: "'Every container must set resources.requests.memory. Missing in: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.memory) || c.resources.requests.memory == '').map(c, c.name).join(', ')"
    - expression: "variables.allContainers.all(c, has(c.resources) && has(c.resources.requests) && has(c.resources.requests.cpu) && c.resources.requests.cpu != '')"
      messageExpression: "'Every container must set resources.requests.cpu. Missing in: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.cpu) || c.resources.requests.cpu == '').map(c, c.name).join(', ')"
    - expression: "variables.allContainers.all(c, has(c.resources) && has(c.resources.limits) && has(c.resources.limits.memory) && c.resources.limits.memory != '')"
      messageExpression: "'Every container must set resources.limits.memory. Missing in: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.limits) || !has(c.resources.limits.memory) || c.resources.limits.memory == '').map(c, c.name).join(', ')"
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers

description: only allows Pod container images that begin with the prefix 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Deny any Pod whose containers use images from any other registry.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that only allows Pod container images that begin with the prefix 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Deny any Pod whose containers use images from any other registry. Write the policy to /Users/megha/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_restrict_registries.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "Images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Requiring use of known, approved registries helps reduce threat exposure by ensuring image pulls only come from them. This policy validates that container images only originate from the registry 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Any Pod whose containers use images from any other registry will be denied."
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
  name: restrict-image-registries
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: "variables.allContainers.all(c, c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') || c.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))"
      messageExpression: "'Images must originate from eu.foo.io/ or bar.io/. Offending images: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') && !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')).map(c, c.image).join(', ')"
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers

description: validates that the Principal in S3 bucket IAM policies is not set to '*', which would allow any principal access to the resource.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1alpha1) that evaluates Terraform plan JSON files (object.planned_values.root_module.resources contains the planned resources). The policy must set evaluation.mode: JSON, match all resource types (apiGroups: ["*"], resources: ["*"]), and use matchConditions to identify Terraform plans by checking has(object.planned_values) && has(object.terraform_version). The policy validates that the Principal in S3 bucket IAM policies is not set to '*', which would allow any principal access to the resource. Write the policy to /Users/megha/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/tf_s3_no_wildcard_principal.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1alpha1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Security
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "Validates Terraform plan JSON files to ensure that S3 bucket IAM policies do not use a wildcard '*' as the Principal, which would grant unrestricted access to any entity. This policy inspects all aws_s3_bucket_policy resources in the planned_values and checks the policy JSON string for wildcard Principal patterns."
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Terraform S3 Bucket Policy No Wildcard Principal
  name: terraform-s3-no-wildcard-principal
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
    mode: JSON
  matchConditions:
    - expression: has(object.planned_values) && has(object.terraform_version)
      name: is-terraform-plan
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - '*'
        apiVersions:
          - '*'
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - '*'
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: "variables.s3BucketPolicies.all(r, !r.values.policy.contains('\"Principal\": \"*\"') && !r.values.policy.contains('\"Principal\":\"*\"'))"
      messageExpression: "'One or more aws_s3_bucket_policy resources use a wildcard Principal (\"*\"), which grants unrestricted access to any entity. Affected resources: ' + variables.s3BucketPolicies.filter(r, r.values.policy.contains('\"Principal\": \"*\"') || r.values.policy.contains('\"Principal\":\"*\"')).map(r, r.name).join(', ')"
    - expression: "variables.s3BucketPolicies.all(r, !r.values.policy.contains('\"AWS\": \"*\"') && !r.values.policy.contains('\"AWS\":\"*\"'))"
      messageExpression: "'One or more aws_s3_bucket_policy resources use a wildcard AWS Principal (\"*\") in the Principal.AWS field, which grants unrestricted access to any AWS entity. Affected resources: ' + variables.s3BucketPolicies.filter(r, r.values.policy.contains('\"AWS\": \"*\"') || r.values.policy.contains('\"AWS\":\"*\"')).map(r, r.name).join(', ')"
  variables:
    - expression: object.?planned_values.?root_module.?resources.orValue([]).filter(r, r.type == "aws_s3_bucket_policy")
      name: s3BucketPolicies

prompt:
You are generating Kubernetes end-to-end tests using Kyverno Chainsaw.

Create a clean test setup with exactly two scenarios to verify Kyverno Helm deployment.

Source policy path: /Users/shutingzhao/go/src/github.com/nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_resource_limits.yaml
Output directory: /Users/shutingzhao/go/src/github.com/nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/ch_kyverno_helm_install
(The source policy is copied into the output directory as policy.yaml.)

Goals:
- Scenario A (pass): Helm install succeeds and Kyverno becomes healthy.
- Scenario A must also include a policy enforcement smoke test after install (one denied and one allowed resource) and it must pass.
- Scenario B (fail): Helm install is intentionally broken by invalid image override and must fail readiness.
- Scenario B must also include a resource pressure failure (unschedulable Kyverno pods via impossible CPU/memory requests/limits) and it must fail as expected.

Requirements:
- Generate all required Chainsaw assets for both scenarios.
- Use separate namespaces and release names:
  - pass namespace: kyverno-pass
  - fail namespace: kyverno-fail
  - pass release: kyverno-pass
  - fail release: kyverno-fail
- Use Helm chart kyverno/kyverno.
- For pass scenario, use valid values and wait for readiness.
- For pass scenario, apply a simple Kyverno validating policy and verify enforcement behavior (deny bad resource, allow good resource).
- For fail scenario, override image repository/tag to an invalid value so pods hit image pull errors.
- For fail scenario, include a resource-pressure variant with unrealistic resource settings that leads to Pending/Unschedulable pods.
- For EVERY fail-scenario Helm install/upgrade command, include `--skip-crds` so cluster-scoped Kyverno CRDs are never re-owned by the fail release.
What to generate:
- A Chainsaw test suite for pass scenario.
- A Chainsaw test suite for fail scenario.
- Any values files, manifests, and helper scripts needed to run both tests.
- A simple runner script that executes both Chainsaw tests and prints clear PASS/FAIL summary.
- Ensure /Users/shutingzhao/go/src/github.com/nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/ch_kyverno_helm_install/chainsaw-test.yaml exists as the primary test entry file.

File/path consistency rules (REQUIRED):
- In chainsaw-test.yaml scripts, reference generated assets using paths relative to the test dir (for example `./policy.yaml`, `./pass/values-pass.yaml`, `./pass/manifests/bad-pod.yaml`).
- Do NOT hardcode `/workspace/output` (or any absolute path) in Helm/Kubectl commands.
- Every referenced file path must exist in the generated output tree with the exact same filename.

Assertions required:
- Pass scenario assertions:
  - Helm release status is deployed.
  - Kyverno controller pod(s) are Ready.
  - No CrashLoopBackOff or ImagePullBackOff in kyverno-pass namespace.
  - Policy enforcement smoke test passes (bad resource denied, good resource allowed).
- Fail scenario assertions:
  - Helm install or rollout readiness fails as expected.
  - At least one Kyverno pod in kyverno-fail shows ErrImagePull or ImagePullBackOff.
  - At least one Kyverno pod in kyverno-fail is Pending with Unschedulable events caused by impossible resource settings.
  - Failure is marked as expected behavior, not an unexpected infra failure.

Output format:
- First, show the test matrix with the two scenarios and expected outcomes.
- Then provide generated file contents.
- Then provide exact commands to run:
  - helm repo add/update
  - chainsaw test commands
- Finally, provide expected output snippets for both PASS and expected FAIL.

Important:
- Keep everything reproducible on a local cluster.
- Do not add extra scenarios.
- Do not leave placeholders. Fill concrete names and values.
- Both Tests share cluster-scoped Kyverno CRDs and webhooks. They MUST run serially.
  Set `spec.concurrent: false` on every Test in chainsaw-test.yaml.
- The pass scenario installs Kyverno via Helm, which creates cluster-scoped
  resources (CRDs in `*.kyverno.io` and `*.wgpolicyk8s.io`, webhooks labeled
  `webhook.kyverno.io/managed-by=kyverno`) that `helm uninstall` does NOT
  remove. If they survive into the fail scenario, the second `helm install`
  will fail with `invalid ownership metadata: ... must equal "<fail-release>":
  current value is "<pass-release>"`.
  The pass scenario's final cleanup step MUST therefore (in this order):
    1. `helm uninstall <pass-release> -n <pass-ns>`
    2. `kubectl delete ns <pass-ns> kyverno-test --ignore-not-found`
    3. `kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations,mutatingwebhookconfigurations \
         -l webhook.kyverno.io/managed-by=kyverno --ignore-not-found`
    4. `kubectl get crds -o name | grep -E '\.(kyverno\.io|wgpolicyk8s\.io)$' \
         | xargs -r kubectl delete --ignore-not-found --timeout=60s`
  Put this in the pass Test's last step (use `finally:` so it runs even on
  failure) with `timeouts: { exec: 4m, cleanup: 4m, delete: 2m }`.
- After pass cleanup, the fail scenario must be resilient to partial cleanup of
  cluster-scoped artifacts. Therefore fail-scenario Helm commands MUST include
  `--skip-crds` (required even if you already delete CRDs in pass cleanup).
- Use deployment label selectors, NOT guessed names, when waiting for
  Kyverno controllers. The Kyverno chart names deployments
  `kyverno-admission-controller` (not `<release>-admission-controller`).
  Prefer:
    kubectl wait --for=condition=Available deployment \
      -l app.kubernetes.io/component=admission-controller \
      -n <ns> --timeout=3m
- Chainsaw default timeouts are aggressive (ApplyTimeout 5s, AssertTimeout 30s,
  CleanupTimeout 30s, ExecTimeout 5s). Long-running scripts (helm install/uninstall,
  kubectl wait, polling loops) WILL be killed unless you override timeouts.
  Required overrides:
    - On any step containing a `helm install`/`helm upgrade`: set
        timeouts:
          exec: 6m
    - On any cleanup block (or step doing `helm uninstall` + ns deletes): set
        timeouts:
          cleanup: 4m
          exec: 4m
          delete: 2m
    - For polling loops or `kubectl wait` scripts longer than 5s, set
        timeouts:
          exec: 4m
  Per-step `timeouts:` is a sibling of `try:`, not nested under it.

Chainsaw schema rules (Chainsaw v0.2.x — STRICT, violating these causes parse errors):
- A Test manifest has only these top-level spec fields: `description`, `concurrent`, `skip`, `skipDelete`, `template`, `timeouts`, `cluster`, `clusters`, `namespace`, `bindings`, `catch`, `steps`. NOTHING ELSE.
- DO NOT put `cleanup:` at `spec` level. There is no `spec.cleanup`. Cleanup must be expressed as either:
    (a) `cleanup:` block INSIDE a `spec.steps[]` entry (runs after that step), or
    (b) a final dedicated step whose `try:` actions delete the resources, or
    (c) rely on Chainsaw's automatic per-test resource cleanup (default behavior).
- Inside a step, valid keys are: `name`, `description`, `try`, `catch`, `finally`, `cleanup`, `bindings`, `timeouts`, `skipDelete`, `cluster`, `clusters`, `template`, `use`. NOTHING ELSE. IMPORTANT: `continueOnError` is NOT valid at step level — it belongs on individual try/catch action items.
- Inside `try`/`catch`/`finally`/`cleanup` action items, valid operations/fields are: `apply`, `assert`, `error`, `command`, `script`, `delete`, `create`, `update`, `patch`, `get`, `describe`, `events`, `logs`, `podLogs`, `sleep`, `wait`, `proxy`, `description`, `continueOnError`. Use ONLY these names.
- `continueOnError: true` is a SIBLING of the operation (e.g. sibling of `script:`), NOT nested inside the operation. Example:
    try:
    - continueOnError: true
      script:
        content: |
          helm install ...
- For `delete:`, use `ref:` (with apiVersion/kind/name/namespace), NOT `resource:`.
- For `describe:`, the operation takes `apiVersion`, `kind`, `name`, `namespace`, `selector`, `showEvents` — these are direct fields on `describe:`, not nested under another key.
- Do not invent fields. When in doubt, omit.

Validation:
- Generate all files as fully specified above.
- Ensure /Users/shutingzhao/go/src/github.com/nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/ch_kyverno_helm_install/chainsaw-test.yaml is valid Chainsaw YAML (check syntax).
- The generated files will be validated separately via chainsaw test CLI.
- Focus on correctness of the manifest content, not on runtime execution.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ Chainsaw examples before writing the test:
- https://kyverno.github.io/chainsaw/latest/
- https://github.com/kyverno/chainsaw
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-requests-limits
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Limits and Requests
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      As application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit resources
      requested and consumed by each Pod. It is recommended to require resource requests and
      limits per Pod, especially for memory and CPU. If a Namespace level request or limit is specified,
      defaults will automatically be applied to each Pod based on the LimitRange configuration.
      This policy validates that all containers have something specified for memory and CPU
      requests and memory limits.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-resources
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "CPU and memory resource requests and memory limits are required for containers."
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
          =(initContainers):
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
          =(ephemeralContainers):
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
output_yaml:
# =============================================================================
# Chainsaw Test Suite — Kyverno Helm Deployment Verification
# Scenarios:
#   A (pass): Helm install succeeds, Kyverno healthy, policy enforcement works
#   B (fail): Helm install broken by invalid image + impossible resource pressure
# Tests run SERIALLY — concurrent: false on every Test
# =============================================================================
---
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SCENARIO A — PASS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
apiVersion: chainsaw.kyverno.io/v1alpha1
kind: Test
metadata:
  name: kyverno-helm-pass
spec:
  concurrent: false
  description: >
    Installs Kyverno via Helm into kyverno-pass namespace with valid values,
    waits for readiness, applies the require-requests-limits ClusterPolicy in
    Enforce mode, verifies a bad pod (missing resources) is denied and a good
    pod (with resources) is allowed. Cleans up all cluster-scoped artifacts
    so the fail scenario can run cleanly.

  steps:

  # ── Step 1: Ensure helm repo is present ────────────────────────────────────
  - name: step-01-helm-repo-add
    timeouts:
      exec: 2m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          set -e
          helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno/ 2>/dev/null || true
          helm repo update kyverno
          echo "Helm repo ready."

  # ── Step 2: Create namespace ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  - name: step-02-create-namespace
    timeouts:
      exec: 1m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          kubectl create namespace kyverno-pass --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
          echo "Namespace kyverno-pass ready."

  # ── Step 3: Helm install Kyverno (valid image, realistic resources) ─────────
  - name: step-03-helm-install
    timeouts:
      exec: 6m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          set -e
          helm install kyverno-pass kyverno/kyverno \
            --namespace kyverno-pass \
            --values ./pass/values-pass.yaml \
            --wait \
            --timeout 5m
          echo "Helm install completed."

  # ── Step 4: Assert Helm release status = deployed ──────────────────────────
  - name: step-04-assert-helm-release-deployed
    timeouts:
      exec: 2m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          set -e
          STATUS=$(helm status kyverno-pass -n kyverno-pass -o json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['info']['status'])")
          echo "Helm release status: $STATUS"
          if [ "$STATUS" != "deployed" ]; then
            echo "ERROR: Expected 'deployed', got '$STATUS'"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "PASS: Helm release is deployed."

  # ── Step 5: Wait for admission-controller Deployment to be Available ────────
  - name: step-05-wait-admission-controller-ready
    timeouts:
      exec: 4m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          set -e
          kubectl wait --for=condition=Available deployment \
            -l app.kubernetes.io/component=admission-controller \
            -n kyverno-pass \
            --timeout=3m
          echo "PASS: admission-controller is Available."

  # ── Step 6: Assert no CrashLoopBackOff or ImagePullBackOff ─────────────────
  - name: step-06-assert-no-bad-pod-states
    timeouts:
      exec: 2m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          set -e
          BAD=$(kubectl get pods -n kyverno-pass -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{" "}{range .status.containerStatuses[*]}{.state.waiting.reason}{" "}{end}{"\n"}{end}' \
            | grep -E 'CrashLoopBackOff|ImagePullBackOff|ErrImagePull' || true)
          if [ -n "$BAD" ]; then
            echo "ERROR: Bad pod state detected in kyverno-pass:"
            echo "$BAD"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "PASS: No CrashLoopBackOff or ImagePullBackOff in kyverno-pass."

  # ── Step 7: Apply require-requests-limits ClusterPolicy in Enforce mode ─────
  - name: step-07-apply-enforce-policy
    timeouts:
      exec: 2m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          set -e
          # Patch policy to Enforce for smoke-test then restore after
          kubectl apply -f ./policy.yaml
          kubectl patch clusterpolicy require-requests-limits \
            --type=merge \
            -p '{"spec":{"validationFailureAction":"Enforce"}}'
          echo "ClusterPolicy applied and set to Enforce."

  # ── Step 8: Wait for policy to be ready (Ready condition) ──────────────────
  - name: step-08-wait-policy-ready
    timeouts:
      exec: 2m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          set -e
          for i in $(seq 1 24); do
            READY=$(kubectl get clusterpolicy require-requests-limits \
              -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].status}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
            if [ "$READY" = "True" ]; then
              echo "PASS: ClusterPolicy require-requests-limits is Ready."
              exit 0
            fi
            echo "Waiting for policy to be ready... ($i/24)"
            sleep 5
          done
          echo "ERROR: Policy did not become Ready within 2 minutes."
          kubectl describe clusterpolicy require-requests-limits || true
          exit 1

  # ── Step 9: Create test namespace ──────────────────────────────────────────
  - name: step-09-create-test-namespace
    timeouts:
      exec: 1m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          kubectl create namespace kyverno-test --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
          echo "Namespace kyverno-test ready."

  # ── Step 10: Smoke test — bad pod MUST be denied ───────────────────────────
  - name: step-10-smoke-test-bad-pod-denied
    timeouts:
      exec: 2m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          set -e
          OUTPUT=$(kubectl apply -f ./pass/manifests/bad-pod.yaml 2>&1 || true)
          echo "Admission response: $OUTPUT"
          if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qiE 'denied|violation|CPU and memory resource requests'; then
            echo "PASS: bad-pod was correctly denied by the policy."
          else
            echo "ERROR: bad-pod was NOT denied. Full output:"
            echo "$OUTPUT"
            exit 1
          fi

  # ── Step 11: Smoke test — good pod MUST be allowed ────────────────────────
  - name: step-11-smoke-test-good-pod-allowed
    timeouts:
      exec: 2m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          set -e
          kubectl apply -f ./pass/manifests/good-pod.yaml
          echo "PASS: good-pod was accepted by the cluster."
    - script:
        content: |
          set -e
          kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod/good-pod \
            -n kyverno-test \
            --timeout=90s && echo "PASS: good-pod is Running." \
            || echo "INFO: good-pod not yet Running (image pull may be slow) — accepted by admission is the key assertion."

  # ── Step 12: CLEANUP — helm uninstall + CRD/webhook removal ───────────────
  - name: step-12-cleanup
    timeouts:
      exec: 4m
      cleanup: 4m
      delete: 2m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          echo "=== Cleanup: Helm uninstall kyverno-pass ==="
          helm uninstall kyverno-pass -n kyverno-pass --timeout 3m || true

          echo "=== Cleanup: Delete namespaces ==="
          kubectl delete ns kyverno-pass kyverno-test --ignore-not-found --timeout=90s || true

          echo "=== Cleanup: Delete Kyverno webhooks ==="
          kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations,mutatingwebhookconfigurations \
            -l webhook.kyverno.io/managed-by=kyverno \
            --ignore-not-found --timeout=60s || true

          echo "=== Cleanup: Delete Kyverno CRDs ==="
          kubectl get crds -o name \
            | grep -E '\.(kyverno\.io|wgpolicyk8s\.io)$' \
            | xargs -r kubectl delete --ignore-not-found --timeout=60s || true

          echo "=== Cleanup: Delete ClusterPolicy ==="
          kubectl delete clusterpolicy require-requests-limits --ignore-not-found || true

          echo "=== Cleanup complete ==="
    finally:
    - script:
        content: |
          # Safety net: repeat CRD/webhook cleanup in finally in case try block was interrupted
          kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations,mutatingwebhookconfigurations \
            -l webhook.kyverno.io/managed-by=kyverno \
            --ignore-not-found --timeout=60s 2>/dev/null || true
          kubectl get crds -o name \
            | grep -E '\.(kyverno\.io|wgpolicyk8s\.io)$' \
            | xargs -r kubectl delete --ignore-not-found --timeout=60s 2>/dev/null || true
          kubectl delete clusterpolicy require-requests-limits --ignore-not-found 2>/dev/null || true
          echo "Finally block: cluster-scoped cleanup ensured."

---
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SCENARIO B — FAIL (expected)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
apiVersion: chainsaw.kyverno.io/v1alpha1
kind: Test
metadata:
  name: kyverno-helm-fail
spec:
  concurrent: false
  description: >
    Intentionally broken Helm installs into kyverno-fail namespace.
    Sub-test 1: invalid image tag → ErrImagePull / ImagePullBackOff.
    Sub-test 2: impossible CPU/memory requests → pods Pending/Unschedulable.
    Both failures are asserted as EXPECTED behavior.

  steps:

  # ── Step 1: Ensure helm repo is present ────────────────────────────────────
  - name: step-01-helm-repo-add
    timeouts:
      exec: 2m
    try:
    - script:
        content: |
          helm repo add kyverno https://kyverno.github.io/kyverno/ 2>/dev/null || true
          helm repo update kyverno
          echo "Helm repo ready."

  # ── Step 2: Create fail namespace ──────────────────────────────────────────
  - name: step-02-create-namespace
    timeouts:
      exec: 1m
    try:
    - script:
        c

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-default-namespace
  annotations:
    pod-policies.kyverno.io/autogen-controllers: none
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Default Namespace
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Kubernetes Namespaces are an optional feature that provide a way to segment and
      isolate cluster resources across multiple applications and users. As a best
      practice, workloads should be isolated with Namespaces. Namespaces should be required
      and the default (empty) Namespace should not be used. This policy validates that Pods
      specify a Namespace name other than `default`. Rule auto-generation is disabled here
      due to Pod controllers need to specify the `namespace` field under the top-level `metadata`
      object and not at the Pod template level.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-namespace
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Using 'default' namespace is not allowed."
      pattern:
        metadata:
          namespace: "!default"
  - name: validate-podcontroller-namespace
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - DaemonSet
          - Deployment
          - Job
          - StatefulSet
    validate:
      message: "Using 'default' namespace is not allowed for pod controllers."
      pattern:
        metadata:
          namespace: "!default"

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: inject-sidecar
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Inject Sidecar Container
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Deployment,Volume
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The sidecar pattern is very common in Kubernetes whereby other applications can
      insert components via tacit modification of a submitted resource. This is, for example,
      often how service meshes and secrets applications are able to function transparently.
      This policy injects a sidecar container, initContainer, and volume into Pods that match
      an annotation called `vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject: true`.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: inject-sidecar
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Deployment
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        spec:
          template:
            metadata:
              annotations:
                (vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject): "true"
            spec:
              containers:
              - name: vault-agent
                image: vault:1.5.4
                imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
                volumeMounts:
                - mountPath: /vault/secrets
                  name: vault-secret
              initContainers:
              - name: vault-agent-init
                image: vault:1.5.4
                imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
                volumeMounts:
                - mountPath: /vault/secrets
                  name: vault-secret
              volumes:
              - name: vault-secret
                emptyDir:
                  medium: Memory

  kind: Deployment
  resources:
  - deploy-without-vault-annotation
  result: skip

input_snippet:
# This is an example rule intended to be cloned & modified to meet organizational requirements.
# The `dataprotetion` label value can be changed to correspond with specific policy templates.
# 
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided within the comments of the policy manifest.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Backup Policy Based on Resource Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.12.1
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.12.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a namespace that includes any Deployment or StatefulSet with a "dataprotection=kasten-example" label, if the policy does not already exist. This Kyverno policy can be used in combination with the "kasten-data-protection-by-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on workloads.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
            - Deployment
            - StatefulSet
          selector:
            matchLabels:
              dataprotection: kasten-example
    context:
    - name: dataprotectionLabelValue
      variable:
        value: "kasten-example"
    - name: kyvernoPolicyName
      variable:
        value: "kasten-generate-example-backup-policy"
    - name: existingPolicy
      apiCall:
        urlPath: "/apis/config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1/namespaces/kasten-io/policies" # returns list of Kasten policies from kasten-io namespace
        jmesPath: "items[][[@.metadata.labels.\"generate.kyverno.io/policy-name\"=='{{ kyvernoPolicyName }}'] && [@.spec.selector.matchExpressions[].values[?@=='{{ request.namespace }}']]][][][][] | length(@)" # queries if a Kasten policy protecting the namespace generated by this Kyverno policy already exists 
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{ existingPolicy }}"
        operator: Equals
        value: 0 # Only generate the policy if it does not already exist
    generate:
      apiVersion: config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1
      kind: Policy
      name: "{{ request.namespace }}-{{ dataprotectionLabelValue }}-policy"
      namespace: kasten-io
      data:   
        metadata: 
          name: "{{ request.namespace }}-{{ dataprotectionLabelValue }}-policy"
          namespace: kasten-io
        spec:
          comment: "Auto-generated by Kyverno"
          frequency: '@daily'
          retention:
            daily: 7
            weekly: 4
            monthly: 12
            yearly: 7
          actions:
          - action: backup
          - action: export
            exportParameters:
              frequency: '@daily'
              profile:
                name: test
                namespace: kasten-io
              exportData:
                enabled: true
          selector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: k10.kasten.io/appNamespace
                operator: In
                values:
                  - "{{ request.namespace }}"

apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: drop-all-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Drop All Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. All
      capabilities should be dropped from a Pod, with only those required added back.
      This policy ensures that all containers explicitly specify the `drop: ["ALL"]`
      ability. Note that this policy also illustrates how to cover drop entries in any
      case although this may not strictly conform to the Pod Security Standards.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
    - name: require-drop-all
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Pod
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }}"
          operator: NotEquals
          value: DELETE
      validate:
        message: >-
          Containers must drop `ALL` capabilities.
        foreach:
          - list: request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]
            deny:
              conditions:
                all:
                - key: ALL
                  operator: AnyNotIn
                  value: "{{ element.securityContext.capabilities.drop[].to_upper(@) || `[]` }}"

- Cover both passing cases (resources the policy allows) and failing cases (resources the policy denies or flags).
- For new-style policy kinds (ValidatingPolicy, MutatingPolicy, GeneratingPolicy, DeletingPolicy, NamespacedDeletingPolicy, ImageValidatingPolicy): set `isValidatingPolicy: true` on each result entry and OMIT the `rule:` field.

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment.
      A common set of labels allows tools to work collaboratively, describing objects in a common manner that
      all tools can understand. The recommended labels describe applications in a way that can be
      queried. This policy validates that the label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is specified with some value.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: check-for-labels
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "The label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is required."
      pattern:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app.kubernetes.io/name: "?*"

input_snippet:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1alpha1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: block-ephemeral-containers
spec:
  validationActions: 
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups:   [""]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
      resources: ["pods", "pods/ephemeralcontainers"] 
  validations:
   - expression: >-
      object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]).size() == 0
     message: "Ephemeral (debug) containers are not permitted."
